incentive.statement: A Lesson 11 typed paradigm decision must remain authoritative through application, higher voice layers, selected Result, and finite surface generation; the entered source stem must never replace the selected paradigm member.

coordinator.responsibility: Trace and repair the first typed-stem continuity break, audit sibling irregular routes through the same path, and keep unrelated active presentation work intact.

outcome: General-past nemi selects typed nen through every active and higher-voice Result path; one typed semantic mood/tense contract owns recognized values and mood compatibility; shell, ledger, normalizer, direct application, restored state, and Lesson 11 lexeme gates cannot diverge or silently authorize fabricated input.

responsibilities.statement: Preserve the final typed Lesson 11 predicate stem and morphological environment whenever a later VNC layer consumes an earlier authorized frame, and enforce one typed semantic mood/tense vocabulary before Lesson 11 contextual authorization.

actors.contribution: Preserved the selected Lesson 11 member and added a typed semantic vocabulary plus fail-closed recognition, mood compatibility, and indicative lexeme-cell enforcement.

responsibilities.statement: Ensure the application result selects the canonical machinery frame and never rebuilds later operations from the entered source stem when a typed target already exists.

actors.contribution: Verified direct application selects nen and that the existing higher-voice general-past checks retain their typed morphological environment.

responsibilities.statement: Consume the typed semantic mood/tense vocabulary, validate actual shell and Canvas-ledger values, and retain invalid restored requests as blocked typed state rather than authorizing a fallback.

actors.contribution: Replaced handwritten normalizer and mood inventories with the canonical semantic contract; added actual shell/ledger inventory validation and blocked-state continuity for restored invalid requests.

responsibilities.statement: Prove active nemi general past, sibling Lesson 11 stem changes, higher voice composition, hostile nearby stems, selected Result, and browser surface continuity.

actors.contribution: Proved actual shell/ledger/contract parity, poisoned-inventory rejection, invalid engine/application/restored-state rejection, four Lesson 11 sibling routes, higher-voice compatibility, and the valid 3sg result.

responsibilities.statement: Record the target, discovered bypass, audit evidence, and closure without claiming unrelated dirty paths.

actors.contribution: Recorded the normalizer bypass, cache-busted delivery, focused evidence, and unrelated alignment debt.

contracts.invariant: Recognized semantic input, mood compatibility, and contextual Lesson 11 availability are typed and fail closed; semantic tense remains distinct from morphological realization.

retirements.removalEvidence: The shared tense normalizer now preserves general-past; selected Result receives the application frame with typed nen, and cache-versioned entry 048 delivers the canonical contract and repaired module graph together.

closure.detail: The entered source stem can no longer replace the selected Lesson 11 typed paradigm member, and fabricated semantic mood or tense input cannot authorize a fallback result.

evidence.detail: Lesson 11 plus continuity passed 22/22; application passed 30/30; matched UI suites passed 405/405; grammar-contract registry passed 43/43; syntax, runtime manifest 75/75, and diff checks passed.

The Lessons 20-22 suite retained all general-past higher-voice formula checks; its sole 29/30 failure is an unrelated pre-existing generated-formation wording expectation in the concurrent presentation work.

Cache-versioned entry and child graph 048 reported authorized shell/ledger semantic inventory parity and produced #0-0(nen)ca+0-0# and Nenca. for 3sg (nemi) general past with zero warning or error logs.
Architecture metrics remained exactly at baseline; the gate is red for the inherited live-board expansion and overlapping unrelated active-action paths.
